title: Carludovica costaricensis
intro: Carludovica costaricensis, also known as the Q'uqungo plant, is a species of flowering plant native to Central America, belonging to the family Cyclanthaceae. The Q'uqungo plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Carludovica costaricensis include saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Carludovica costaricensis is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, often used to treat respiratory conditions, fever, and gastrointestinal issues.
